ending:
Randidly wins the semifinal by draining the Breaking Dawn contender's borrowed ether, dismantling his sun image, and exploiting the injuries accumulated during their fight. The effort destroys his ability to continue. A hostile projection tries to seize him, but the Steel Feather leader escapes with him at the cost of an arm. The retired veteran then teleports Randidly away, and a returning ally transfers him to his own world to recover and grow stronger.

At Shal's inn, Lucretia's possession attack has already killed the male spear attendant through the merchant's controlled body. The merchant survives, grieving, and admits that he killed the attendant while possessed. The female attendant leaves to investigate a relative's disappearance that she suspects is connected to the Endless Heat threat. Shal wakes and confirms that Lucretia is his birth mother. After the summoned Earth Golem badly injures the Endless Heat master and departs, Shal and the wounded Steel Feather leader remain together to confront him. Lucretia has entered Randidly's soul world with a captive soul and other resources, so the ivory spear's restraint does not settle her fate. The tournament final, Shal's confrontation, and Dearden's political crisis remain unresolved.
in_short: Randidly travels to Dearden to preserve Shal's Spear Phantom tradition by placing in a hostile regional tournament. He survives coercion inside his own soul, rebuilds his inner world, develops new spear techniques, and reaches the semifinal despite political interference. Lucretia captures Shal, bargains for Randidly's Aether, and attacks his companions through possession. A hunger swarm exposes Dearden's willingness to sacrifice lesser defenders, while Randidly's attendants fight beside him and build their own futures. Randidly defeats the Breaking Dawn contender after collapsing his sun image, but the victory leaves him unconscious and severely injured. The male attendant is killed by the possessed merchant. Shal wakes and learns Lucretia is his birth mother, then joins the wounded Steel Feather leader against the Endless Heat master. Randidly is evacuated to his own world to recover, while Lucretia remains insecurely contained inside his soul world and the conflicts in Dearden continue.

recaps.text: Previously: Randidly Ghosthound entered the wider System as a classless fighter and became Shal's spear disciple. His development left him with unusual Aether ties to Annie, Sam, Mrs. Hamilton, Alana, Arbor, and Thorn on Earth. Those human and plant connections remain part of the living network attached to his inner power. Shal carries the Spear Phantom inheritance while suffering from ether sickness and pursuing old injuries connected to his family. Randidly's progress has not resolved the larger danger surrounding the System or the uncertainty around people missing from his earlier life.

Randidly travels with three attendants to Dearden, where he must reach the regional tournament's top eight for Shal. He studies engraving, trains the Spear Phantom image, and discovers that a living emerald wisp has emerged from his sealed soul skill. The wisp soon divides, while golden locks prevent him from examining the source. Shal remains away, following a lead on Lucretia and trying to recover from ether sickness.

The tournament preliminaries begin with a shared-weight endurance trial designed to turn qualifier entrants against one another. Randidly conserves his deeper abilities and chooses to outlast the field. Officials exploit a desperate competitor with spearman's breath, then kill him when he begins becoming Forsaken. As the trial continues after its first death, four emerald wisps now move within Randidly's inner space.

Randidly wins the full twenty-four-hour endurance challenge after an ice construct kills his last rival. Completing his path brings him face to face with the hidden entity that placed the golden locks. It claims to have cultivated him because his classlessness and altered Aether created an independent crossroads, then demands control of that inner source in exchange for a refuge beyond the System. Randidly refuses.

The entity seals his senses and tortures him, destroying the emerald wisps that try to help. Their sacrifice guides him back to reciprocal connections with Annie, Sam, Mrs. Hamilton, Alana, Arbor, and Thorn. Using the life and meaning flowing through those bonds, he breaks free long enough to dismantle a prepared weapon seed. He rebuilds his soul skill as a clockwork inner world with distinct regions and peoples, completing its assimilation as the entity breaches his defenses.

Randidly's rebuilt soul skill becomes Four Shaded Clockwork World, although he fears its surviving weapon core may still serve the hidden entity. He gains Aether Connection, a consensual link that could ease the isolation of his inner source but would make him valuable to anyone seeking energy. Shal warns him that styles might imprison a person with such a gift.

The second preliminary requires every entrant to learn and rapidly improve Flexibility. Because the skill spirit rejects classless Randidly, he falsely claims to know an equivalent and creates one himself by studying the movement of beings inside his soul world. His new personal skill carries him through the deadline and helps him defeat a preliminary rival. Seventy-six survivors join one hundred selected style disciples in the regional tournament field.

The regional tournament uses a triple-elimination structure that favors established styles and denies preliminary entrants time to recover. Randidly defeats a Blooming Flower disciple, then suffers two severe losses against elite opponents before surviving his fourth match. Shal raises the required result from top eight to top four and gives Randidly access to his father's spear diary. From its incomplete components, Randidly creates Spear Phantom's Embrace, a rare technique joining movement, feints, image, and mental pressure.

Randidly uses the new technique to remain in the field, then defeats the red-haired rival whose devouring energy weakens combat images. His companions turn their food stall into a large tournament business while his female attendant grows rapidly through training. When the heavily favored contender concedes without fighting him, Randidly advances into the final thirty-two.

Randidly's attendants become entangled in family and style pressure, while the Northern Region spearwoman offers Randidly brutal training for her own political purpose. Shal refuses an Aether connection from Randidly, fears echoes of Lucretia's influence, and leaves Dearden. Lucretia captures him without Randidly's knowledge. Randidly responds to the apparent abandonment by committing to repeated training defeats and improving his regeneration.

The Engraving Guild begins threatening Randidly over the source of his unauthorized equipment. In the tournament proper, the red-haired finalist demonstrates image-erasing energy, and Randidly faces a muscular favorite he previously lost to. By combining roots, fire, mobility, disease, and spearwork without allowing a stable exchange, Randidly forces the favorite to concede. He reaches the top sixteen and disrupts the result Dearden's major styles expected.

Dearden's major styles warn Randidly not to advance beyond the top eight, while the defeated muscular favorite reports hearing an offer of stolen power. The Devourer's disciple privately gives Randidly a disputed account of Shal's family: Lucretia grants three dangerous gifts, and a transferable devouring rune helped cause an old disaster. She also admits that her orders require her to stop the Spear Phantom style from reaching the top eight.

In their match, the disciple reveals near-Artisan skill with a whip-spear and devouring energy that erodes images, abilities, and flesh. Randidly confines her movement with roots, exploits her weakness to heat, and inflicts a devastating spear strike. She chooses self-immolation rather than continued bondage. Randidly wins the needed top-eight result, but Lucretia captures the disciple's soul and keeps the unresponsive Shal in her custody.

A gray spark from the defeated disciple's remains triggers a Forsaken outbreak and summons a hunger swarm to Dearden. Randidly joins low-status defenders on the western wall and learns that major styles routinely shelter while expendable spear users die. He and his female attendant hold the wall with fire, roots, spearwork, and transferred ether. A Central Domain representative destroys the main swarm mass, but only after defenders are killed or transformed and fighting spreads through the city.

While recovering from severe leg wounds, Randidly completes another Ash path and is taken into the Patron of Ash's trial. Its heat and visions probe his unresolved guilt, family history, and missing friends. He risks approaching the central sphere instead of merely enduring. Black material remakes his skeleton, and he returns as an anointed of Ash with Ashes to Ashes and the pain-projecting Anguish.

Randidly trains his new Ash abilities while learning more about the marriage politics binding the strongest northern styles. Lucretia visits him and confirms that she holds Shal, seeking access to Randidly's unusually pure Aether. He considers a limited trade but refuses an open-ended bond. Shal remains trapped in a dreamlike struggle against a shade connected to the Spear Phantom founder.

In the top-eight match, the Steel Feather finalist's speed and force-wave techniques badly damage Randidly and crack his obsidian spear. He extends Phantom's Embrace to borrow her momentum, then creates an ash-and-volcano technique capable of meeting her strongest attack. The new spear image briefly reaches Artisan quality. Randidly survives her final committed strike, redirects its force, and wins when she collapses. He advances after demonstrating his first Artisan-level spear technique.

Randidly bargains Aether to Lucretia for Shal's release and the cancellation of her claims over him. Shal returns physically but remains unconscious, caught between conflicting battle intents. The payment leaves Lucretia faintly connected to Randidly. Before he can settle the consequences, three Central Region Artisans ambush him because the Spear Phantom style reached the top eight.

Randidly is outmatched, and one attacker shatters the obsidian shell of his spear. Its engraved bone core survives as a usable weapon. Randidly forces Lucretia to share the effects of Anguish and Torment through their tether, then uses an inspired fire attack to scatter his attackers. The Northern Region spearwoman who trained him arrives, identifies the ambushers as foreign agents, and prevents them from finishing him.

The Northern Region spearwoman kills one ambusher, while two escape and Dearden's authorities offer no explanation. The Endless Heat master begins traveling personally toward the city, claiming that Shal is tied to a bloodline secret. Shal continues fighting the founder's shade in his dream and develops a hybrid style instead of simply accepting the inheritance.

Randidly discovers that the bone spear absorbs Aether and stores emotions and meaning associated with the Spear Phantom founder. He reproduces two of its outer engravings on daggers, creating dangerous tools that exchange life and meaning at severe cost. The inherited memories suggest a bargain with Lucretia and a family disaster, but their reliability remains uncertain. Randidly uses the spear to enter Shal's dreamspace, tells his mentor that he is needed, and leaves the engraved daggers for him.

Randidly prepares for his semifinal against the Breaking Dawn contender, whose political marriage campaign also threatens Randidly's life. The female attendant struggles with Randidly's expected departure, while the male attendant plans a separate future at the border. Shal remains asleep.

During the semifinal preparations, Lucretia attacks Shal's inn through possessed bodies. The merchant attendant is controlled by a red-eyed soul and turned against the male attendant. The male attendant's defensive iron image develops into armor as he fights through severe wounds, but Lucretia's forces break his defense. The possessing soul takes direct control of the merchant's final strike and decapitates him. The merchant remains alive and traumatized after the killing while the attack continues around Shal.

Lucretia draws heavily on her tether to Randidly while an Endless Heat master attacks Shal's inn. She opens Randidly's soul world and releases an ancient Earth Golem warrior to defend Shal. In the arena, Randidly fights the Breaking Dawn contender, drains ether through the bone spear, survives a killing thrust with Ashes to Ashes, and collapses the opponent's dominant sun image by extracting its meaning.

Randidly then opens his soul world to Lucretia, draws her meaning inside, and pins it with the spear. Left unarmed, he breaks the contender's weapon and knocks him unconscious, winning the semifinal despite crippling injuries. A hostile projection arrives to seize him, but the Steel Feather leader carries him away. At the inn, Shal wakes. Lucretia confirms that she is his birth mother and enters Randidly's soul world voluntarily with a captive soul and other resources, leaving her true containment uncertain.

The Steel Feather leader protects civilians while escaping the hostile projection with unconscious Randidly, losing an arm in the pursuit. Randidly creates one last opening with his new sun-stilling image and gives his rescuer a small amount of pure Aether. The retired veteran receives Randidly and uses a teleportation scroll to remove him from Dearden. A returning ally then transfers him to his own world, where he is to recover and develop before coming back.

Shal wakes fully and learns that Lucretia is his birth mother. After the Earth Golem badly injures the Endless Heat master and departs, Shal and the Steel Feather leader stand together against the master. The female attendant finds the male attendant's body. The grieving merchant admits that he killed him while possessed. She leaves to investigate a relative's disappearance that she suspects may involve the Endless Heat threat. Randidly is alive but unconscious, Lucretia remains insecurely confined within his soul world, and the tournament final and battles in Dearden remain unresolved.
